metadata.purpose: Complete API feature reference for comparison with AWS Bedrock API

max_tokens.description: Maximum number of tokens to generate before stopping. Different models have different maximum values.

messages.description: Input messages. Each has role (user|assistant) and content (string or content blocks array)

system.description: System prompt. No system role in messages; use this parameter instead.

temperature.description: Randomness. 0.0 for analytical, 1.0 for creative. Not fully deterministic even at 0.0.

top_p.description: Nucleus sampling. Use either temperature or top_p, not both.

top_k.description: Only sample from top K options for each token.

stop_sequences.description: Custom text sequences that cause stop. stop_reason becomes stop_sequence.

tools.description: Tool definitions the model may use.

cache_control.description: Top-level cache control. Auto-applies to last cacheable block.

container.description: Container identifier for reuse across requests

response_format.content: array of content blocks (text, tool_use, thinking)

1_messages_api.python_sdk_example:
client = anthropic.Anthropic()
message = client.messages.create(
    model='claude-opus-4-6',
    max_tokens=1024,
    messages=[{'role': 'user', 'content': 'Hello'}]
)

description.description: Detailed description of what the tool does

input_schema.description: JSON Schema defining the tool's input parameters

tool_choice_options.auto: Model decides whether to use tools (default)
tool_choice_options.any: Model must use one of the available tools
tool_choice_options.tool: Model must use the specific named tool
tool_choice_options.disable_parallel_tool_use: boolean - if true, outputs at most one tool_use block

tool_result_format.content: string or array of content blocks

notes: Tool names must match regex: ^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{1,64}$
Tool descriptions should be very detailed for best results
With extended thinking, tool_choice only supports auto or none
Parallel tool use enabled by default unless disable_parallel_tool_use is true

best_practices: Place images before text in prompts
Use supported formats: JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP
Ensure images are clear and legible
Resize images to no more than 1568px on any edge for optimal latency
Images under 200px on any edge may degrade quality
limitations: Limited spatial reasoning (clock faces, chess positions)
Not designed for complex diagnostic medical scans

message_start.description: First event, contains message object with empty content

4_streaming.python_sdk_example:
with client.messages.stream(
    max_tokens=1024,
    messages=[{'role': 'user', 'content': 'Hello'}],
    model='claude-opus-4-6',
) as stream:
    for text in stream.text_stream:
        print(text, end='', flush=True)
4_streaming.python_sdk_get_final_message:
with client.messages.stream(...) as stream:
    message = stream.get_final_message()

enabled.budget_tokens: integer, minimum 1024, must be < max_tokens (unless interleaved)

adaptive.note: Model decides when and how much to think. Recommended for Opus 4.6.

supported_models.claude-sonnet-4-6: Both manual and adaptive, supports interleaved mode

display_options.summarized: Default. Returns key thinking points. Claude 4+ returns summarized (not full) thinking.
display_options.omitted: Empty thinking field, signature only. Faster time-to-first-text-token. Same cost.

tool_use_constraints: tool_choice only supports auto (default) or none with thinking enabled
any or specific tool forcing causes errors
Must preserve thinking blocks in multi-turn tool use
Cannot toggle thinking mid-turn during tool loops

interleaved_thinking.sonnet_4_6: Beta header required OR adaptive thinking

5_extended_thinking.billing: Billed for FULL thinking tokens generated, not the summarized output

6_prompt_caching.endpoint: POST /v1/messages (with cache_control on blocks or top-level)

automatic.description: Top-level cache_control auto-applies to last cacheable block

explicit_breakpoints.description: Place cache_control on individual content blocks

cacheable_content: Tool use blocks and tool results
Thinking blocks (as part of previous assistant turns)
6_prompt_caching.cache_hierarchy: tools -> system -> messages (changes invalidate that level and all subsequent)
notes: Exact matching required - 100% identical content
Organization-level cache isolation (moving to workspace-level Feb 5, 2026)
Concurrent requests: cache only available after first response begins
Supported on Claude API and Azure AI Foundry (preview); Bedrock/Vertex coming later
6_prompt_caching.python_sdk_example:
response = client.messages.create(
    model='claude-opus-4-6',
    max_tokens=1024,
    cache_control={'type': 'ephemeral'},
    system='Long system prompt...',
    messages=[{'role': 'user', 'content': 'Question'}]
)

7_batch_processing.pricing: 50% of standard API prices for all models
notes: NOT eligible for Zero Data Retention (ZDR)
Scoped to Workspace
Each request processed independently - can mix types
Consider 1-hour cache TTL for better cache hits with batches

8_token_counting.pricing: FREE - no token cost, only RPM limits
notes: Token count is an ESTIMATE - actual may differ slightly
May include system-added tokens (not billed)
Separate rate limits from message creation
Does NOT use prompt caching logic
Thinking blocks from previous assistant turns are ignored
Eligible for Zero Data Retention (ZDR)
8_token_counting.python_sdk_example:
response = client.messages.count_tokens(
    model='claude-opus-4-6',
    system='You are a scientist',
    messages=[{'role': 'user', 'content': 'Hello, Claude'}]
)
print(response.input_tokens)  # e.g. 14

document_block_structure.title: optional string
document_block_structure.context: optional string - additional context not cited

how_it_works: Each page converted to an image
Text extracted and provided alongside page image
Claude analyzes both text and images
9_pdf_support.supported_platforms: Direct API, Google Vertex AI, Amazon Bedrock (with caveats)

bedrock_specific_notes.converse_api_without_citations: Basic text extraction only - cannot see images/charts

best_practices: Place PDFs before text in requests
Ensure text is clear and legible
Enable prompt caching for repeated analysis

10_citations.endpoint: POST /v1/messages (with citations enabled on document blocks)
10_citations.how_to_enable: Set citations.enabled=true on each document block

pdf.chunking: Automatic sentence chunking on extracted text

custom_content.chunking: No additional chunking - your blocks used as-is

char_location.cited_text: exact text (NOT counted as output tokens)

optional_document_fields.title: Passed to model but NOT citable
optional_document_fields.context: Passed to model but NOT citable

token_costs.input: Slight increase due to system prompt additions and chunking
token_costs.output: cited_text does NOT count towards output tokens
token_costs.multi_turn: cited_text also NOT counted as input tokens when passed back

10_citations.streaming: citations_delta event type contains citation objects

event_flow: 1. message_start - Contains Message object with empty content
2. content_block_start - For each content block (text, tool_use, thinking)

special_events.error: Error during streaming, may be recoverable

notes: Each SSE includes event type and JSON data
Ping events sent periodically as keepalive
SDKs provide high-level streaming helpers (text_stream, etc.)
For large max_tokens, SDKs may require streaming to avoid HTTP timeouts

global_notes.sdk_initialization: client = anthropic.Anthropic()  # reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var
